prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response: The old lighthouse keeper had seen countless fogs roll in from the sea, each one as thick and impenetrable as the last. He knew the routines, the steady turns of the light, the crash of the waves against the rocks below. But this night was different. A figure walked out of the fog, a rusted key in their hand, and approached the lighthouse. The keeper stepped outside, ready to turn the stranger away, but they vanished into the fog once more, leaving the key at his feet. With a trembling hand, the keeper picked it up and found it fit the lock of the old storage room, a room he had never been able to open. Inside, he found letters, journals, and maps that told a story he could never have imagined, a tale of love, loss, and a treasure hidden within the rocks of the lighthouse itself.
